reasoning: Based on multiple contemporaneous reports, two primary suspects were arrested in late October 2025 in connection with the October 19th Louvre robbery. Crucially, authorities state these two men have "partially admitted" to their roles and are specifically identified as the individuals who broke into the gallery (while accomplices waited outside). Furthermore, forensic evidence, specifically DNA found on a broken display case inside the Louvre, links at least one of these suspects directly to the physical act of the robbery. The market criteria explicitly requires "any perpetrators involved in the physical robbery... apprehended" and "clear evidence that they were directly involved in the actual robbery itself." Since these specific suspects entered the gallery and left DNA on the display cases, they satisfy this condition perfectly. As of the forecast date (Nov 9), they have been formally charged and remain in detention, making it exceedingly unlikely there will be a "consensus of credible reporting implying that they were likely not the culprit," especially given the DNA evidence and partial confessions. Since only a single arrested physical perpetrator is required for a YES resolution, the conditions have been unequivocally met.
